items.name: Isentropic Area-Mach Relation
items.description: Isentropic nozzle area ratio as a function of Mach number.
assumptions: Quasi-one-dimensional isentropic flow.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Choked Mass Flux
items.description: Choked Mass Flux
assumptions: One-dimensional isentropic converging nozzle at critical condition (M=1 at throat).
Perfect gas with constant gamma and R.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(compressible::choked_mass_flux::equation())
    .target_g_star()
    .given_p0("500000 Pa")
    .given_t0("300 K")
    .given_gamma(1.4)
    .given_r("287 J/(kg*K)")
    .value()?;

items.name: Fanno Density Ratio
items.description: Fanno-flow density ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic, constant-area duct flow with wall friction.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Fanno Friction Length Parameter
items.description: Fanno-flow friction length parameter to the sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic, constant-area duct flow with wall friction.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Fanno Pressure Ratio
items.description: Fanno-flow static pressure ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic, constant-area duct flow with wall friction.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Fanno Stagnation Pressure Ratio
items.description: Fanno-flow stagnation pressure ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic, constant-area duct flow with wall friction.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

variables.name: Stagnation pressure ratio to star state

items.name: Fanno Temperature Ratio
items.description: Fanno-flow static temperature ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic, constant-area duct flow with wall friction.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Fanno Velocity Ratio
items.description: Fanno-flow velocity ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic, constant-area duct flow with wall friction.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Isentropic Density Ratio
items.description: Isentropic Density Ratio
assumptions: Isentropic perfect-gas flow with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Isentropic Pressure Ratio
items.description: Isentropic Pressure Ratio
assumptions: One-dimensional isentropic flow of a calorically perfect gas.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Isentropic Temperature Ratio
items.description: Isentropic Temperature Ratio
assumptions: Isentropic perfect-gas flow with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Mach Angle
items.description: Mach angle as a function of Mach number for supersonic flow.
assumptions: Calorically perfect gas, isentropic context.
Mach angle is defined for M >= 1.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Normal Shock Density Ratio
items.description: Density ratio across a normal shock.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic normal shock.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Normal Shock Downstream Mach Number
items.description: Downstream Mach number after a normal shock.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic normal shock.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Normal Shock Static Pressure Ratio
items.description: Static pressure ratio across a normal shock.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic normal shock.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Normal Shock Stagnation Pressure Ratio
items.description: Stagnation pressure ratio across a normal shock.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic normal shock.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Normal Shock Temperature Ratio
items.description: Static temperature ratio across a normal shock.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, adiabatic normal shock.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Oblique Shock Downstream Mach
items.description: Downstream Mach number from downstream normal Mach and oblique-shock geometry.
assumptions: Attached oblique shock geometry with beta > theta.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Oblique Shock Normal Upstream Mach
items.description: Normal component of upstream Mach number for oblique shock geometry.
assumptions: Attached oblique shock geometry.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Oblique Shock Theta-Beta-M Relation
items.description: Oblique-shock flow deflection angle from upstream Mach number and shock angle.
assumptions: Attached oblique shock, inviscid perfect-gas flow.
Upstream Mach number is supersonic (M1 > 1).

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Prandtl-Meyer Expansion Angle
items.description: Prandtl-Meyer angle as a function of Mach number for supersonic expansion.
assumptions: Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.
Defined for M >= 1 in supersonic expansion flow.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

references.source: NASA Glenn Research Center, Prandtl-Meyer Angle

items.name: Rayleigh Density Ratio
items.description: Rayleigh-flow density ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, constant-area, frictionless flow with heat transfer.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Rayleigh Pressure Ratio
items.description: Rayleigh-flow static pressure ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, constant-area, frictionless flow with heat transfer.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Rayleigh Stagnation Pressure Ratio
items.description: Rayleigh-flow stagnation pressure ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, constant-area, frictionless flow with heat transfer.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

variables.name: Stagnation pressure ratio to star state

items.name: Rayleigh Stagnation Temperature Ratio
items.description: Rayleigh-flow stagnation temperature ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, constant-area, frictionless flow with heat transfer.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

variables.name: Stagnation temperature ratio to star state

items.name: Rayleigh Temperature Ratio
items.description: Rayleigh-flow static temperature ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, constant-area, frictionless flow with heat transfer.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Rayleigh Velocity Ratio
items.description: Rayleigh-flow velocity ratio relative to sonic star state.
assumptions: One-dimensional, steady, constant-area, frictionless flow with heat transfer.
Calorically perfect gas with constant gamma.

source.source: Anderson, Modern Compressible Flow: With Historical Perspective

items.name: Circular Pipe Flow Area
items.description: Circular Pipe Flow Area
assumptions: Cross-section is circular and unobstructed.

source.source: Fox, McDonald, and Pritchard, Introduction to Fluid Mechanics

items.name: Colebrook-White Friction Factor
items.description: Implicit turbulent pipe-flow friction factor relation.
assumptions: Fully developed turbulent internal flow.
Incompressible flow behavior.

source.source: Fox, McDonald, and Pritchard, Introduction to Fluid Mechanics

references.note: Turbulent flow in pipes with rough walls.

items.name: Continuity Mass Flow
items.description: Continuity Mass Flow
assumptions: One-dimensional mean properties represent cross-section averages.

source.source: Fox, McDonald, and Pritchard, Introduction to Fluid Mechanics

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(fluids::continuity_mass_flow::equation())
    .target_m_dot()
    .given_rho("998 kg/m3")
    .given_a("0.1 m2")
    .given_v("3 m/s")
    .value()?;

items.name: Darcy-Weisbach Pressure Drop
items.description: Darcy-Weisbach Pressure Drop
assumptions: Fully developed internal flow in a constant-diameter conduit.

source.source: Fox, McDonald, and Pritchard, Introduction to Fluid Mechanics

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(fluids::darcy_weisbach_pressure_drop::equation())
    .target_delta_p()
    .given_f(0.02)
    .given_l("10 m")
    .given_d("0.1 m")
    .given_rho("1000 kg/m3")
    .given_v("3 m/s")
    .value()?;

examples.label: convenience_delta_p
convenience_f
convenience_l
convenience_d
convenience_rho
convenience_v

items.name: Incompressible Orifice Mass Flow
items.description: Incompressible Orifice Mass Flow
items.unicode: m_dot = C_d · A · √(2 · ρ · Δ p)
items.ascii: m_dot = C_d * A * sqrt(2 * rho * delta_p)
assumptions: Incompressible single-phase flow through a sharp-edged restriction.

source.source: Fox, McDonald, and Pritchard, Introduction to Fluid Mechanics

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(fluids::orifice_mass_flow_incompressible::equation())
    .target_m_dot()
    .given_c_d(1.0)
    .given_a("1 m2")
    .given_rho("1 kg/m3")
    .given_delta_p("0.5 Pa")
    .value()?;

examples.label: convenience_c_d

items.name: Reynolds Number
items.description: Reynolds Number
assumptions: Internal-flow characteristic length is represented by hydraulic diameter.

source.source: Fox, McDonald, and Pritchard, Introduction to Fluid Mechanics

examples.label: typed_builder_context
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve_with_context(fluids::reynolds_number::equation())
    .fluid(eng_fluids::water().state_tp("300 K", "1 bar")?)
    .target_re()
    .given_v("2.5 m/s")
    .given_d("0.1 m")
    .value()?;

items.name: Plane-Wall Conduction Heat Rate
items.description: Plane-Wall Conduction Heat Rate
assumptions: One-dimensional steady conduction through a homogeneous plane wall.

source.source: Incropera et al., Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(heat_transfer::conduction_plane_wall_heat_rate::equation())
    .target_q_dot()
    .given_a("2 m2")
    .given_t_h("330 K")
    .given_t_c("280 K")
    .given_l("1 m")
    .value()?;

examples.label: typed_builder_context
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve_with_context(heat_transfer::conduction_plane_wall_heat_rate::equation())
    .material(eng_materials::stainless_304().temperature("350 K")?)
    .target_q_dot()
    .given_a("2 m2")
    .given_t_h("330 K")
    .given_t_c("280 K")
    .given_l("1 m")
    .value()?;

items.name: Convection Heat Transfer Rate
items.description: Convection Heat Transfer Rate
assumptions: Newton's law of cooling with uniform h and area.

source.source: Incropera et al., Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(heat_transfer::convection_heat_rate::equation())
    .target_q_dot()
    .given_h("35 W/(m2*K)")
    .given_a("3 m2")
    .given_t_s("400 K")
    .given_t_inf("300 K")
    .value()?;

items.name: Log-Mean Temperature Difference
items.description: Log-Mean Temperature Difference
items.unicode: Δ T_{lm} = (Δ T_1 - Δ T_2) / ln(Δ T_1 / Δ T_2)
assumptions: End-point temperature differences remain positive and unequal.

source.source: Incropera et al., Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer

items.name: Conduction Thermal Resistance
items.description: Conduction Thermal Resistance
assumptions: One-dimensional steady conduction with constant properties.

source.source: Incropera et al., Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(heat_transfer::thermal_resistance_conduction::equation())
    .target_r_th()
    .given_l("0.1 m")
    .given_k("10 W/(m*K)")
    .given_a("0.2 m2")
    .value()?;

items.name: Convection Thermal Resistance
items.description: Convection Thermal Resistance
assumptions: Uniform convection coefficient over the area.

source.source: Incropera et al., Fundamentals of Heat and Mass Transfer

items.name: Ideal Characteristic Velocity
items.description: Ideal Characteristic Velocity
assumptions: Ideal nozzle flow with calorically perfect combustion products.

source.source: Sutton and Biblarz, Rocket Propulsion Elements

items.name: Ideal Specific Impulse
items.description: Ideal Specific Impulse
assumptions: Vacuum-equivalent ideal performance relation.

source.source: Sutton and Biblarz, Rocket Propulsion Elements

items.name: Ideal Thrust Coefficient
items.description: Ideal Thrust Coefficient
items.ascii: C_f = sqrt((2 * gamma^2 / (gamma - 1)) * pow(2 / (gamma + 1), (gamma + 1) / (gamma - 1)) * (1 - pow(p_e_p_c, (gamma - 1) / gamma))) + (p_e_p_c - p_a_p_c) * A_e_A_t
assumptions: Ideal isentropic nozzle expansion.

source.source: Sutton and Biblarz, Rocket Propulsion Elements

items.name: Thrust From Mass Flow and Effective Exhaust Velocity
items.description: Thrust From Mass Flow and Effective Exhaust Velocity
assumptions: Pressure-thrust contribution is embedded in effective exhaust velocity.

source.source: Sutton and Biblarz, Rocket Propulsion Elements

items.name: Axial Normal Stress
items.description: Axial Normal Stress
assumptions: Uniform axial loading and stress distribution.

source.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

items.name: Beam Bending Stress
items.description: Beam Bending Stress
assumptions: Linear elastic beam bending with small strains.

source.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(structures::beam_bending_stress::equation())
    .target_sigma_b()
    .given_m("5000 N*m")
    .given_c("0.05 m")
    .given_i("5e-6 m4")
    .value()?;

items.name: Euler Buckling Critical Load
items.description: Euler Buckling Critical Load
assumptions: Ideal slender column with elastic buckling.

source.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

examples.label: typed_builder_context
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve_with_context(structures::euler_buckling_load::equation())
    .material(eng_materials::stainless_304().temperature("350 K")?)
    .target_p_cr()
    .given_i("8e-6 m4")
    .given_k(1.0)
    .given_l("2 m")
    .value()?;

items.name: Thin-Wall Hoop Stress
items.description: Thin-Wall Hoop Stress
assumptions: Thin-wall approximation is valid (t << r).
Material behavior remains in the elastic membrane-stress regime.

source.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

references.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(structures::hoop_stress::equation())
    .target_sigma_h()
    .given_p("2.5 MPa")
    .given_r("0.2 m")
    .given_t("8 mm")
    .value()?;

items.name: Thin-Wall Longitudinal Stress
items.description: Thin-Wall Longitudinal Stress
assumptions: Thin-wall cylindrical vessel behavior.

source.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(structures::longitudinal_stress_thin_wall::equation())
    .target_sigma_l()
    .given_p("2.5 MPa")
    .given_r("0.2 m")
    .given_t("8 mm")
    .value()?;

items.name: Circular Shaft Torsion Stress
items.description: Circular Shaft Torsion Stress
assumptions: Circular shaft in Saint-Venant torsion.

source.source: Roark's Formulas for Stress and Strain

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(structures::shaft_torsion_stress::equation())
    .target_tau()
    .given_t("1200 N*m")
    .given_r("0.04 m")
    .given_j("1.6e-5 m4")
    .value()?;

items.name: Ideal Gas Law (Density Form)
items.description: Ideal Gas Law (Density Form)
assumptions: Thermally and calorically ideal gas behavior.
Single-phase gas state.

source.source: Moran et al., Fundamentals of Engineering Thermodynamics

examples.label: typed_builder_units
examples.code:
let value = eq
    .solve(thermo::density::equation())
    .target_p()
    .given_rho("1.17683 kg/m3")
    .given_r("287 J/(kg*K)")
    .given_t("300 K")
    .value()?;

items.name: Ideal Gas Law (Mass-Volume Form)
items.description: Ideal Gas Law (Mass-Volume Form)
assumptions: Thermally and calorically ideal gas behavior.
Gas is in equilibrium at a single representative state.

source.source: Moran et al., Fundamentals of Engineering Thermodynamics

    .given_r("287 J/(kg*K)")
    .given_t("300 K")
    .value()?;
